domain: statistical mechanics (spin glass / replica symmetry breaking, glassy aging)
specific_mechanism: Spin glass aging: after a sudden change (field cooling, temperature quench), a spin glass relaxes toward equilibrium with power-law (not exponential) relaxation times that grow with the system's age. Caused by metastable energy landscape with many shallow minima separated by varying barrier heights. The system explores the landscape via thermal activation; relaxation requires barrier crossings, and the available barrier crossings change with age.
llm_application: Diagnose long-running LLM agent behavior with spin-glass-style aging analysis: measure relaxation times to novel inputs as a function of deployment age. Predict power-law vs exponential scaling. Use glassy landscape picture to design fine-tune protocols that navigate the landscape (rather than just push gradient).
novelty_claim: Spin-glass models of neural networks is one of the most established statistical-mechanics-to-deep-learning analogies: Replica Symmetry Breaking in NN (1803.06442), Neural Networks as Spin Models (2408.06421), Loss Landscapes through Spin Glass Theory (2407.20724), From Spin Glasses to Learning (Springer), Spin-glass models of neural networks. Software aging in LLM systems is studied (2510.24188). Mode collapse as metastable-region confinement (2605.00435). The spin-glass-aging-as-LLM-deployment-aging framing is in a heavily occupied umbrella.
